seoTitle: How to Remove White Space Between Pages in Google Docs
metaDescription: Collapse the gray gap between pages in Google Docs with a quick double-click shortcut or the Show print layout toggle for smoother, uninterrupted scrolling.
intro: The gap between pages in Google Docs is useful for print preview but can slow down reading or editing long documents. This guide covers the fast double-click shortcut that collapses those gaps instantly, plus the View menu toggle that controls the same setting globally. Either method gives you a smoother, uninterrupted scrolling experience.

steps.heading: Find the page break boundary
steps.body: Hover your cursor over the gray line dividing two pages to locate the exact spot where the gap sits.

steps.heading: Double-click to collapse the gap
steps.body: Double-click directly on the page break boundary to instantly hide the header, footer, and white space between pages.

steps.heading: Review your compacted layout
steps.body: Scroll through your document to confirm the text now flows continuously without visible page breaks interrupting it.

steps.heading: Or use the View menu toggle
steps.body: Open the View menu and toggle Show print layout off to achieve the same collapsed effect across your whole workspace.

steps.heading: Restore page borders when needed
steps.body: Repeat the double-click or menu toggle to bring back full page borders whenever you need to check print alignment.

faq.question: Does collapsing white space affect how my document prints?
faq.answer: No, this setting only changes your on-screen view; the actual page breaks and print layout remain unaffected.

faq.question: Will other collaborators see my document without page gaps too?
faq.answer: No, the print layout toggle is a personal display setting, so it only affects your own view, not what others see when they open the file.

faq.question: Is there a keyboard shortcut for toggling this setting?
faq.answer: The quickest method is double-clicking the page break line directly, since there isn't a dedicated keyboard shortcut for this toggle.

faq.question: Why would I want to remove the white space at all?
faq.answer: It's useful for reading long documents, reviewing code or scripts, or skimming manuscripts without constant scrolling interruptions from page gaps.
